definition_compliance.source: Anthropic Grace et al. 2026 + TerminalBench-2 + agent-harness survey

criteria.six_components_evaluated: partial — execution env / tool integration / context mgmt covered; scope negotiation / loop mgmt / verification implicit

phase_1_capability_comparison.label: capability comparison, NOT harness engineering

results.outcome: user_cancel after timeout — MCP stdio boot too slow for read-only Q&A

phase_1_capability_comparison.verdict: Sonnet most efficient (default tier confirmed). Opus accurate but ToolSearch overhead penalty for simple lookups. Haiku accurate but 57% more tool calls than Sonnet. Codex unsuitable for one-shot read-only Q&A.

phase_2_orchestrator_worker_pattern.label: real harness-engineering trajectory per Anthropic orchestrator-worker pattern
phase_2_orchestrator_worker_pattern.end_to_end_task: Author docs/release-notes/v1.9.52-harness-eval.md from scratch via orchestrator-worker dispatch with full trajectory + pass/fail gate

roles.task: bounded scan: git log -10, ls release-notes, wc release-notes, ls schema-snapshot artifacts, git tag list
roles.notes: Bounded scan tier — perfect fit. 5/5 read-only Bash with zero overhead.

roles.task: Synthesize 7-acceptance-criteria draft from worker output + Phase 1 measurement + parent context
roles.notes: Self-reported ~3k tokens vs trajectory 33k — 11x drift. Cross-check self-stats with trajectory log mandatory. Otherwise clean: 1 Read followed by full draft synthesis.

roles.task: Score 7 ACs with binary 0.0/0.5/1.0 + actionable repair hints + verdict
roles.notes: 0 tool calls — pure critic role on draft text. Identified 3 placeholder-table issues + 1 self-stat anomaly accurately.

roles.task: Apply final markdown patch to release notes file
roles.skip_reason: Phase 1 timeout in same read-only environment + single Write call by parent is cheaper than Codex MCP boot

repairs: AC2: distinguished Grace et al. 2026 (model+harness pair definition) vs TerminalBench-2 (trajectory + binary pass/fail methodology)
AC3: rendered actual 4-row capability table with Codex timeout reason
AC4: corrected Sonnet self-stat (3k→33k, 15s→77s) + filled Evaluator row with real numbers (0/32k/32s/PARTIAL→PASS)
AC5: rendered 4-row tier guidance matrix with when-to-use + when-to-avoid

limitations: single-arm v1.9.52 only — no v1.9.50 A/B in this run
n=1 task per tier (capability) and n=1 end-to-end task (orch-worker)
task selection biased toward O8a-introduced fields
Codex skip in Phase 2 prevents specialist tier evaluation
self-report token drift 11x in Sonnet — accurate only after parent cross-check
Phase 1 is capability comparison NOT harness engineering
observer effect: orchestrator records own run

plumbing_changes_in_release.claude_md_global: added Subagent Model Selection + Tool Budget Calibration + 3 learned rules (harness eval definition, capability vs harness eng, surface 4 priority)
plumbing_changes_in_release.claude_md_project: added Model × CodeLens Workflow Matrix + Subagent CodeLens scoping guide
plumbing_changes_in_release.settings_json: no changes (dead env avoided per learned rule on unused abstractions)

next_steps: v1.9.50 binary side-by-side A/B (requires session restart)
expand to n>=20 tasks per tier for statistical significance
Codex specialist evaluation in implementation loop (edit+build+test) instead of read-only Q&A
reproduce orch-worker pattern in other task domains (review, refactor, spec design)
